_description: Second eval set, designed to test whether the retrieval bottleneck observed on the first PDF (dense theoretical math) is content-specific or systemic. This paper is applied ML (LLMs, RAG, reinforcement learning) with cleaner prose and less math notation. All questions are in English to match the document language. Source PDF: data/pdfs/2605.11974v1.pdf (Chu et al. — Towards Order Fairness: Mitigating LLMs Order Sensitivity through Dual Group Advantage Optimization, arXiv:2605.11974v1).

questions.question: What is the core problem with LLMs that DGAO aims to solve?
What is the institutional affiliation of the authors?
What two new metrics does the paper introduce to evaluate order stability?
Where is the source code for DGAO available?
What does PAFT stand for in Table 1?
What type of reinforcement learning algorithm is DGAO?
Which DeepSeek model was trained with GRPO?
What well-known phenomenon does the paper reference about LLMs attending to certain parts of the context?
On which three task types do the experiments show that DGAO improves performance?
How does the paper distinguish on-policy from off-policy algorithms?
questions.reference: whether the learning samples are generated by the current model being optimized

questions.question: What is the main weakness of off-policy methods like DPO that the paper highlights?
What negative effect does the paper identify when using SFT with data augmentation to improve order stability?
questions.reference: simply fine-tuning models through data augmentation may cause models to generate consistently incorrect answers

questions.question: What is the paper's headline claim about the novelty of using reinforcement learning here?
questions.reference: first time reinforcement learning is used
